Useful when transitioning between scenes.",

      "full_doc": "Enables or disables automatic time-of-day ambient colour cycling.\n\nWhen `true`, `update()` calls `compute_color_from_time()` each\nframe and overwrites `ambient.color`. When `false`, the colour\nset via `setAmbientColor` is used as a static tint.\n@param enabled : boolean",

      "full_doc": "Sets the number of cloud shadow blobs rendered each frame.\n\nHigher counts produce denser, more uniform coverage. Each blob\nis positioned pseudo-randomly based on its index, so the layout\nis consistent across frames.\n@param count : number",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",

      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 487,

      "full_doc": "Enables or disables the scrolling cloud shadow overlay.\n\nCloud shadows are rendered as soft blob-shaped shadow blobs\ndrifting horizontally across the scene. Configure their\nappearance with `setCloudCount`, `setCloudScale`, and\n`setCloudOpacity`.\n@param enabled : boolean",

      "full_doc": "Enables or disables atmospheric fog.\n@param enabled : boolean\nEnables or disables the atmospheric fog overlay.\n\nWhen enabled, a translucent colour rectangle is blended over the\nfull scene every frame. Configure thickness via `setFogDensity`\nand colour via `setFogColor`.",

    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setHeatHazeEnabled",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setHeatHazeEnabled",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Enables or disables UV-distortion heat shimmer.",
      "full_doc": "Enables or disables UV-distortion heat shimmer.\n\nWhen enabled, the renderer applies a time-animated sine wave\ndisplacement to the scene's UV coordinates, simulating the\nwavering distortion seen above hot surfaces.\n@param enabled : boolean",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 570,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setHeatHazeIntensity",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setHeatHazeIntensity",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Sets the heat haze distortion intensity.",
      "full_doc": "Sets the heat haze distortion intensity.\n\nControls the peak UV displacement in pixels. 0.2â€“2.0 gives\nsubtle mirage shimmer; higher values are suitable for extreme\nheat or magical warp effects.\n@param intensity : number",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 587,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setTimeOfDay",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setTimeOfDay",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Sets the time-of-day value that drives ambient colour cycling.",
      "full_doc": "Sets the time-of-day value that drives ambient colour cycling.\n\nThe value is in 24-hour floating-point format (0.0 = midnight,\n12.0 = noon, 24.0 wraps to midnight again). Has no visible\neffect unless ambient cycling is enabled via `setAmbientEnabled`.\n@param hour : number",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 178,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setVignetteEnabled",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setVignetteEnabled",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Enables or disables screen-edge darkening (vignette).",
      "full_doc": "Enables or disables screen-edge darkening (vignette).\n\nThe vignette grades from transparent at the screen centre to\nnear-black at the corners. Useful for suggesting restricted\nvision, dramatic tension, or a cinematic look.\n@param enabled : boolean",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 608,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setVignetteStrength",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setVignetteStrength",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Sets the vignette darkening strength (0.0â€“1.0).",
      "full_doc": "Sets the vignette darkening strength (0.0â€“1.0).\n\n0.0 is invisible; 0.5 is a gentle filmic border; 1.0 crushes\ncorners to near-black.\n@param strength : number",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 624,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setWeather",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setWeather",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Sets the active weather type by name.",
      "full_doc": "Sets the active weather type by name.\n\nValid names: `\"none\"`, `\"rain\"`, `\"snow\"`, `\"hail\"`, `\"dust\"`,\n`\"leaves\"`, `\"ash\"`, `\"pollen\"`. The new type takes effect on the\nnext `update` call â€” existing particles are not cleared, so there\nmay be a brief mix of old and new particle behaviors. Returns a\nLua error if `name` is unrecognised.\n@param weather_type : string",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 218,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setWeatherEnabled",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setWeatherEnabled",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Enables or disables the weather particle spawner.",
      "full_doc": "Enables or disables the weather particle spawner.\n\nWhen disabled, no new particles are spawned but existing ones\ncontinue to move and age until they leave the screen. Set the\nweather type to `\"none\"` first if you want an immediate cutoff.\n@param enabled : boolean",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 291,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setWeatherIntensity",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setWeatherIntensity",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Sets the weather particle density.",
      "full_doc": "Sets the weather particle density.\n\nControls both the maximum particle count (up to 200) and the\nspawn rate. 0.0 stops spawning; 1.0 produces the maximum\nparticle density for the current weather type.\n@param intensity : number",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 241,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setWindDirection",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setWindDirection",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Sets the wind direction as an angle in radians.",
      "full_doc": "Sets the wind direction as an angle in radians.\n\n0.0 blows particles to the right; `math.pi / 2` blows downward.\nCombined with `setWindSpeed`, this drifts all live particles\neach frame by the wind velocity vector.\n@param angle : number",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 258,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "setWindSpeed",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:setWindSpeed",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Sets the wind speed in pixels per second.",
      "full_doc": "Sets the wind speed in pixels per second.\n\nAdds a global velocity offset to every live weather particle each\nframe. 0.0 disables wind drift.\n@param speed : number",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 274,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "triggerLightning",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:triggerLightning",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Triggers a one-shot lightning flash.",
      "full_doc": "Triggers a one-shot lightning flash.\n\nFires a brief hard white flash (default 0.15 s). Calling while\na flash is in progress restarts it from full brightness.\nCustomise the flash colour first with `setLightningColor`.",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 680,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "overlay",
      "name": "update",
      "lua_name": "Overlay:update",
      "owner_type": "Overlay",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Advances all active overlay subsystems by `dt` seconds.",
      "full_doc": "Advances all active overlay subsystems by `dt` seconds.\n\nDrives weather particle spawning and culling, flash and shake\ndecay, fade interpolation, cloud scroll accumulation, and\nlightning deactivation. Call exactly once per frame in\n`luna.update(dt)` before reading any derived state such as\n`getShakeOffset` or `isFlashing`.\n@param dt : number",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 66,
      "file": "src/lua_api/overlay_api.rs"

      "lua_name": "luna.physics.addPulleyJoint",
      "owner_type": "",
      "kind": "function",
      "description": "Adds a pulley joint linking two bodies through a rope of fixed total length.",
      "full_doc": "Adds a pulley joint linking two bodies through a rope of fixed total length.\n\n# Parameters\n- `world` ÔÇö World ID.\n- `body1` ÔÇö First body ID.\n- `body2` ÔÇö Second body ID.\n- `gx1`, `gy1` ÔÇö Ground anchor for body1 in world coordinates.\n- `gx2`, `gy2` ÔÇö Ground anchor for body2 in world coordinates.\n- `ax1`, `ay1` ÔÇö Body1 attachment point in local coordinates.\n- `ax2`, `ay2` ÔÇö Body2 attachment point in local coordinates.\n- `ratio` ÔÇö Pulley ratio (1.0 = symmetric).\n# Returns\nNew joint ID.",
      "params": "- `world` ÔÇö World ID.\n- `body1` ÔÇö First body ID.\n- `body2` ÔÇö Second body ID.\n- `gx1`, `gy1` ÔÇö Ground anchor for body1 in world coordinates.\n- `gx2`, `gy2` ÔÇö Ground anchor for body2 in world coordinates.\n- `ax1`, `ay1` ÔÇö Body1 attachment point in local coordinates.\n- `ax2`, `ay2` ÔÇö Body2 attachment point in local coordinates.\n- `ratio` ÔÇö Pulley ratio (1.0 = symmetric).",
      "returns": "New joint ID.",
      "line": 1013,
      "file": "src/lua_api/physics_api.rs"
    },

      "params": "- `world` ÔÇö World ID.\n- `body1` ÔÇö First body ID.\n- `body2` ÔÇö Second body ID.\n- `x1`, `y1` ÔÇö Anchor on body1 in local coordinates.\n- `x2`, `y2` ÔÇö Anchor on body2 in local coordinates.\n- `maxLength` ÔÇö Maximum allowed distance in world units.",

      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Sets the colour grading brightness multiplier.",
      "full_doc": "Sets the colour grading brightness multiplier.\n\nApplied as a uniform scale across all channels before contrast\nand saturation adjustments. 1.0 is neutral; values below 1.0\ndarken the scene; values above 1.0 brighten it.\n# Parameters\n- `value` â€” `number` â€” Brightness multiplier (default 1.0).",

      "owner_type": "PostFxEffect",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Sets the colour grading contrast.",
      "full_doc": "Sets the colour grading contrast.\n\nPivots around mid-grey (0.5). Values above 1.0 push darks darker\nand lights lighter; values below 1.0 flatten the tonal range.\n# Parameters\n- `value` â€” `number` â€” Contrast multiplier (default 1.0).",
      "params": "- `value` â€” `number` â€” Contrast multiplier (default 1.0).",
      "returns": "",

      "params": "- `enabled` â€” `boolean` â€” `true` to activate, `false` to skip.",

    {
      "module": "postfx",
      "name": "isEffectEnabled",
      "lua_name": "PostFxStack:isEffectEnabled",
      "owner_type": "PostFxStack",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Returns `true` if the given effect is enabled within this stack.",
      "full_doc": "Returns `true` if the given effect is enabled within this stack.\n\nQueries the per-position enabled flag in the chain. Returns `false`\nif the effect is not in this stack's registry at all. This is\ndistinct from `effect:isEnabled()`, which reads the enabled flag\nstored directly on the `PostFxEffect` object; both flags must be\n`true` for the pass to execute during `apply()`.\n# Parameters\n- `effect` â€” `PostFxEffect` userdata â€” The effect to query.\n# Returns\n`boolean` â€” `false` if the effect is absent from the chain.",
      "params": "- `effect` â€” `PostFxEffect` userdata â€” The effect to query.",
      "returns": "`boolean` â€” `false` if the effect is absent from the chain.",
      "line": 514,
      "file": "src/lua_api/postfx_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "postfx",
      "name": "remove",
      "lua_name": "PostFxStack:remove",
      "owner_type": "PostFxStack",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Removes a `PostFxEffect` from the stack chain.",
      "full_doc": "Removes a `PostFxEffect` from the stack chain.\n\nIdentifies the effect by pointer equality against the local registry.\nAfter removal all subsequent effects shift down by one position.\nReturns `false` if the effect is not in this stack's registry.\n# Parameters\n- `effect` â€” `PostFxEffect` userdata â€” The effect to remove.\n# Returns\n`boolean` â€” `true` if the effect was found and removed.",
      "params": "- `effect` â€” `PostFxEffect` userdata â€” The effect to remove.",
      "returns": "`boolean` â€” `true` if the effect was found and removed.",
      "line": 433,
      "file": "src/lua_api/postfx_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "postfx",
      "name": "resize",
      "lua_name": "PostFxStack:resize",
      "owner_type": "PostFxStack",
      "kind": "method",
      "description": "Resizes the internal canvas dimensions.",
      "full_doc": "Resizes the internal canvas dimensions.\n\nCall this whenever the window or render target changes size so the\nGPU layer can recreate ping-pong canvases at the correct resolution.\nDoes not remove or invalidate any effects in the chain.\n# Parameters\n- `w` â€” `integer` â€” New canvas width in pixels.\n- `h` â€” `integer` â€” New canvas height in pixels.",
      "params": "- `w` â€” `integer` â€” New canvas width in pixels.\n- `h` â€” `integer` â€” New canvas height in pixels.",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 601,
      "file": "src/lua_api/postfx_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "procgen",
      "name": "cellularAutomata",
      "lua_name": "luna.procgen.cellularAutomata",
      "owner_type": "",
      "kind": "function",
      "description": "Generates a cave-like map using cellular automata.",
      "full_doc": "Generates a cave-like map using cellular automata.\n\n`opts` fields (all optional):\n- `fill`       — `number`  Initial fill probability 0–1 (default 0.45).\n- `iterations` — `number`  Smoothing iterations (default 5).\n- `birth`      — `number`  Live-cell birth threshold (default 4).\n- `survive`    — `number`  Live-cell survival threshold (default 3).\n- `seed`       — `number`  Random seed (default 0).\nReturns a flat Lua table of `w * h` bytes (0 = empty, 1 = wall).\n@param w    number  Grid width.\n@param h    number  Grid height.\n@param opts table   (optional)\n@return table",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 46,
      "file": "src/lua_api/procgen_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "procgen",
      "name": "floodFill",
      "lua_name": "luna.procgen.floodFill",
      "owner_type": "",
      "kind": "function",
      "description": "BFS flood fill on a flat grid of bytes.",
      "full_doc": "BFS flood fill on a flat grid of bytes.\n\nReturns a flat Lua table (same size as `data`) with 1 for filled cells\nand 0 for unfilled cells.\n@param data      table   Flat grid of byte values (1-based).\n@param w         number  Grid width.\n@param h         number  Grid height.\n@param sx        number  Seed column (0-based).\n@param sy        number  Seed row    (0-based).\n@param threshold number  Fill boundary value (default 128).\n@param above     boolean If true fill cells >= threshold; else <= threshold (default false).\n@return table",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 81,
      "file": "src/lua_api/procgen_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "procgen",
      "name": "perlinNoise",
      "lua_name": "luna.procgen.perlinNoise",
      "owner_type": "",
      "kind": "function",
      "description": "Evaluates a single periodic Perlin noise value at `(x, y)`.",
      "full_doc": "Evaluates a single periodic Perlin noise value at `(x, y)`.\n\n`px` and `py` are the tile periods — the noise wraps seamlessly when\ncoordinates advance by one period.\n@param x  number\n@param y  number\n@param px number  X period (tile width in noise space).\n@param py number  Y period (tile height in noise space).\n@return number  Value in [-1, 1].",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 122,
      "file": "src/lua_api/procgen_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "procgen",
      "name": "poissonDisk",
      "lua_name": "luna.procgen.poissonDisk",
      "owner_type": "",
      "kind": "function",
      "description": "Generates Poisson disk sample points using Bridson's algorithm.",
      "full_doc": "Generates Poisson disk sample points using Bridson's algorithm.\n\nReturns a Lua table of `{x, y}` point tables.\n@param w           number  Domain width.\n@param h           number  Domain height.\n@param min_dist    number  Minimum distance between any two points.\n@param max_attempts number (default 30) Candidates per active point.\n@param seed        number  (default 0) Random seed.\n@return table",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 143,
      "file": "src/lua_api/procgen_api.rs"
    },
    {
      "module": "procgen",
      "name": "voronoi",
      "lua_name": "luna.procgen.voronoi",
      "owner_type": "",
      "kind": "function",
      "description": "Generates a Voronoi diagram for a set of seed points.",
      "full_doc": "Generates a Voronoi diagram for a set of seed points.\n\n`pts` is a Lua array of `{x, y}` tables (seed points).\n`opts` fields (all optional):\n- `warp_scale`    — `number`  Warp noise frequency (default 0.1).\n- `warp_strength` — `number`  Warp noise amplitude; 0 = no warp (default 0).\n- `seed`          — `number`  Seed for warp noise (default 0).\nReturns three flat tables of size `w * h`:\n- `regions`   — `u32` index of the closest seed point.\n- `dist`      — `f32` distance to the closest seed.\n- `dist2`     — `f32` distance to the second-closest seed.\n@param w    number\n@param h    number\n@param pts  table\n@param opts table  (optional)\n@return table, table, table",
      "params": "",
      "returns": "",
      "line": 185,
      "file": "src/lua_api/procgen_api.rs"
    },
